chronology.title: First Spanish Echo in Caddo World (1542)
chronology.description: Early recorded Spanish contact memory in Caddo-region narratives.

chronology.title: French Claim Date Anchor (1682)
chronology.description: Symbolic opening threshold for imperial claim versus lived local continuity.

chronology.title: Natchitoches Founded (1714)
chronology.description: French trade-post foundation in Native Red River landscape.

chronology.title: Los Adaes as Capital of Spanish Texas (1729)
chronology.description: Spanish frontier governance consolidation near French borderland.

coreTruthSet.description: Los Adaes is treated as key Spanish counterweight node to French Natchitoches and central borderland hinge.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Los Adaes ... mission-presidio complex specifically to counter the nearby French presence at Natchitoches.

coreTruthSet.description: The proposed 1682 opening is framed as contrast between European legal claim and a Native world still living by its own rhythms.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: A haunting contrast: a European legal act on paper versus a Native world still living by its own rhythms.

coreTruthSet.description: Spanish presence is modeled as background pressure from the beginning rather than late-stage surprise.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Spanish story ... present from the beginning as a pressure in the background.

coreTruthSet.description: Borderland is framed as lived, mixed, and negotiated rather than cleanly divided by imperial lines.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The border was never clean. It was a lived frontier.

coreTruthSet.description: Native experience sequence is framed as contact shocks, then competing empires, then paper transfers not matching lived reality.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: First feels Europeans as disease/rumor/... then competing empires ... then one empire replacing another on paper.

settingAnchors.name: Natchitoches-Los Adaes Borderland Corridor
settingAnchors.description: Core French-Spanish-Native interaction corridor linking missions, presidios, trade, and diplomacy.

continuityConstraints.title: Background Pressure Reveal Structure
continuityConstraints.text: Spanish pressure should be seeded as ambient force early, then progressively clarified as frontier institutions tighten.

continuityConstraints.title: Master Timeline Engine (Five-Column Model)
continuityConstraints.text: Story planning should map date/action/affected groups/lived impact/story use for each turning point in the borderland sequence.

continuityConstraints.title: Native Viewpoint Over Imperial Paper
continuityConstraints.text: Chapter framing should privilege lived Native impacts over formal imperial claims when sequencing historical turns.

openResearchGaps.title: How should legal trade, illicit exchange, and diplomatic necessity be balanced scene-by-scene in the corridor?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to keep frontier realism without flattening political complexity.

openResearchGaps.title: What would local Red River communities plausibly know in each decade about Spanish and French movements?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to prevent anachronistic awareness in character dialogue and decisions.

openResearchGaps.title: Which Spanish/French census and census-substitute sources are strongest for Natchitoches-Los Adaes family-level reconstruction?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to harden population and household claims used in chapter scaffolding.
